document.source_title: Israel to extend F-35I range amid war with Iran
document.author: Tzally Greenberg
document.summary: Israel's defense ministry announced plans to develop a system to extend the range of its F-35I 'Adir' fighter jets. The upgrade will involve adapting external fuel tanks based on the F-16I 'Sufa' design. This announcement comes amid ongoing tensions with Iran and follows a recent Israeli military operation where an F-35I achieved its first air-to-air kill. Israel currently operates 50 F-35I aircraft and is negotiating to expand its fleet significantly.
key_takeaways: Israel to extend the range of F-35I fighter jets.
Development involves adapting external fuel tanks from F-16I design.
Current F-35A range is 1,200 miles; F-16I has a range of 1,300 miles.
Elbit Systems awarded a $34 million contract for development.
Israel operates 50 F-35I jets and is negotiating to purchase 25 more.
Recent military operation resulted in the first air-to-air kill by an F-35.
document.why_it_matters: The extension of the F-35I's range enhances Israel's military capabilities amid ongoing regional tensions, particularly with Iran. This development reflects Israel's commitment to maintaining a technological edge in defense.

claims.claim_text: Israel will develop a system for extending the range of its F-35I fighter jets.
The F-35A has a range of 1,200 miles.
claims.evidence_text: The F-35A — the model on which the Israeli version is based — currently has a range of 1,200 miles, as stated on the manufacturer’s website.

claims.claim_text: Israel operates 50 F-35I aircraft and is negotiating to purchase 25 more.
claims.evidence_text: Israel currently operates 50 F-35I aircraft across two squadrons... announced the opening of negotiations to purchase 25 more.

entities.name: F-35I 'Adir'
entities.description: A fifth-generation multi-mission stealth fighter aircraft used by the Israeli Air Force.
entities.evidence_text: The F-35I “Adir” is a fifth-generation multi-mission stealth fighter aircraft, manufactured by Lockheed Martin.

entities.name: Elbit Systems
entities.description: An Israeli defense electronics company awarded the contract for developing the range extension system.
entities.evidence_text: Elbit Systems was awarded the development contract, valued at approximately $34 million.

entities.name: Lion’s Roar
entities.description: An Israeli military operation that involved airstrikes deep inside Iran.
entities.evidence_text: a month after the Israeli military operation 'Lion’s Roar,' in which the Israeli Air Force struck targets deep inside Iran.
